title: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC
summary: The four Artemis 2 astronauts have arrived in Florida for final preparations ahead of a planned launch as soon as April 1. This marks a key milestone in NASA's Artemis program, which aims to return humans to the Moon.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced the addition of two new associate members, Astrolab and Zeno Power, which focus on planetary mobility and energy solutions for extreme environments. This move highlights CSF's growing role in supporting innovative commercial space companies.

title: ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is set to decide by June on its contributions to NASA's Gateway lunar-orbiting station, following NASA's recent decision to halt work on the project. This move comes as Europe re-evaluates its role in U.S. lunar ambitions amid shifting Artemis program priorities.

title: India’s Bellatrix raises $20 million following overseas expansion drive
summary: India-based Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million to expand production of satellite propulsion systems after securing its first major international commercial customer. The funding follows the company's overseas expansion efforts.

title: Building a hypersonic pathway to orbit with Mark Bigham
summary: The Space Minds podcast features a discussion between host David Ariosto and Mark Bigham, VP of Defense Programs at Longshot Space Technologies Corporation, about building a hypersonic pathway to orbit. Longshot Space Technologies is introduced as a new hardware startup focused on this technology.

title: The ‘ground truth’ gap in AgTech: Why satellites alone can’t save supply chains
summary: Satellite technology has revolutionized precision agriculture and supply chain monitoring in AgTech, but 'ground truth' data remains essential for accuracy. Despite advancements in satellite hardware, launch costs, and AI-driven analytics, satellites alone cannot fully address supply chain challenges.

title: Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion
summary: NASA's proposed changes to its support for commercial space stations have caused concern and confusion among developing companies, according to an industry organization leader.

title: SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit
summary: Canadian startup SBQuantum will send a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit on March 30 aboard a Spire Global satellite launched by SpaceX's Falcon 9. The collaboration involves Spire handling the satellite, ground stations, and data processing for SBQuantum's compact magnetometer.

title: ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service
summary: ispace is revising its lunar lander design and delaying its first mission by its American subsidiary while also announcing plans for a lunar satellite constellation.

title: U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft
summary: U.S. GSSAP satellites have conducted a handoff maneuver to monitor China's Shijian-29 spacecraft in geostationary orbit. Commercial space tracking data indicates continuous observation of the Chinese spacecraft.

title: Astrophotographer captures spectacular photo of Antennae Galaxies dueling in deep space
summary: An astrophotographer captured a stunning image of the Antennae Galaxies merging into a single elliptical galaxy. This rare cosmic event highlights the dynamic processes of galactic collisions.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 203 — China Rising
summary: This Week In Space podcast Episode 203 discusses China's rising influence in space and potential threats to American leadership, featuring Dr. Namrata Goswami from Johns Hopkins University.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' author Andy Weir reveals his favorite scenes in the movie, praises Ryan Gosling's amazing performance, & more (interview)
summary: Andy Weir, author of 'Project Hail Mary,' shares his favorite scenes from the movie adaptation and praises Ryan Gosling's performance in an interview. Weir highlights how Gosling's portrayal added depth to aspects Weir didn't cover in the book.

title: This Celestron Firstscope is my go-to for moonwatching and it's 15% off in Amazon's Spring Sale
summary: The Celestron Firstscope telescope is currently 15% off during Amazon's Spring Sale, making it an affordable option for moonwatching and skywatching for both adults and children.

title: How to watch NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts launch to the moon on April 1
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission is scheduled to launch four astronauts to the moon on April 1 at 6:24 p.m. EDT (2224 GMT). Details on how to watch the live event have been provided.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at Kennedy Space Center ahead of NASA's historic launch around the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts have arrived at Kennedy Space Center ahead of their historic lunar flyby mission, scheduled to launch in five days. This marks a key milestone for the Artemis program, aiming to return humans to the moon.

title: What time is NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch on April 1?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission, the first crewed moon flight in over 50 years, is scheduled for a potential April 1 launch. Live coverage details have been provided for the event.

title: Still waiting for Ahsoka Season 2? Make the wait easier with 20% off this Lego Star Wars Ahsoka Tano Jedi Interceptor
summary: A Lego Star Wars Ahsoka Tano Jedi Interceptor set is being offered at a 20% discount at Walmart, providing a distraction while fans await the release of Star Wars: Ahsoka Season 2.

title: Can humans have babies in space? It may be harder than expected
summary: A new study suggests that mammalian reproduction in space may be challenging due to sperm's difficulty finding an egg in microgravity. This raises concerns about the feasibility of human reproduction in space.

title: Fireball sightings are surging across the US — here's what's really going on
summary: Bright meteor sightings have increased over Ohio, Texas, and Europe, raising questions about whether meteor activity is truly rising or if reporting has simply surged. The phenomenon has sparked public curiosity and scientific interest.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon. His career has focused on enabling spacecraft communication with Earth.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA has selected Intuitive Machines to deliver science and technology payloads to the Moon for $180.4 million under the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission includes seven payloads, five of which are NASA-funded, to advance lunar research.

title: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
summary: NASA announced the 'Blue Marble' Awards, recognizing excellence in environmental management and project execution. The awards highlight achievements in compliance, conservation, and stakeholder partnerships.

title: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
summary: NISAR, a U.S.-Indian Earth observation satellite, captured an image of Mount Rainier on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's data provides detailed surface imagery over the Pacific Northwest.

title: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
summary: The U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR captured an image of Mount St. Helens in Washington on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The image is part of a larger swath of the Pacific Northwest.

title: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
summary: NASA's technology and science demonstrations will launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-16 rideshare mission. The demonstrations aim to test thermal protection systems, improve in-space communications, and enhance understanding of Earth's atmosphere.

title: NASA Names Scientists to Support Lunar South Pole Science
summary: NASA has selected 10 scientists to assist in planning lunar surface science activities for the Artemis program, including instrument deployment and sample collection. These scientists will help shape the scientific objectives for astronauts during future Moon missions.

title: I Am Artemis: Michael Guzman
summary: Michael Guzman, the main propulsion systems engineer for NASA's Artemis II mission, shares insights into his work in a recent audio excerpt. His email signature includes detailed rocket propulsion equations.

title: NextSTEP-3 E: Network Extension for User Continuity and Sustainability (NEXUS) Ka-Band Backward-Compatible Relay Broad
summary: NASA plans to release a Broad Agency Announcement (BAA) under NextSTEP-3 Appendix E for Project NEXUS to acquire a Ka-band backward-compatible relay service, addressing the decline of the aging TDRS system.

title: Artemis II Crew’s Suits
summary: NASA and CSA unveil the Orion Crew Survival System suits for the Artemis II crew. The suits will be worn by Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, Mission Specialist Christina Koch, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen during the upcoming Artemis II test flight.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA revealed detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, marking a significant shift away from its previous Gateway-focused lunar program. The agency is now prioritizing a more direct approach to establishing a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders revealed the mission details.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is rapidly advancing ground infrastructure at Starbase in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starships, highlighting their focus on increasing launch frequency. The ongoing construction and upgrades at the facility demonstrate the company's commitment to scaling operations.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets carrying internet satellites. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from Baikonur Cosmodrome's repaired pad. The mission follows repairs after a previous launch from the same site.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the manufacturing pace of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center. The company is expanding its space industry ambitions with these initiatives.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the crewed lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. The test brings the next Starship launch one step closer to reality.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: Nine launches are scheduled for the week, featuring SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Isar Aerospace's Spectrum. The launches span multiple countries and missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific space industry news is provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team reports that the spacecraft is now operational again.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events aim to inform industry about ESA's planned activities, priorities, and opportunities.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, mandating its suppliers to use the new costing software for financial proposals in ESA Tender Actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky and expensive solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the root cause and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic and human movement. This initiative aims to advance space exploration technologies and educational research.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS network. The company aims to provide stronger, more accurate, and secure Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) signals in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) compared to traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: Jared Isaacman, NASA Administrator, marked 100 days in office with a bold announcement about returning to the Moon. The statement emphasizes an accelerated push for lunar missions.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space primes invested in components for the lunar orbiting station, but recent Artemis program changes have left them at a disadvantage. Europe now faces uncertainty regarding its role in the Artemis program.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's evolving vision for Commercial Low-Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs), as proposed by Administrator Jared Isaacman. The plan has sparked confusion and debate among policymakers.

title: Directed Energy: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The article discusses directed energy as an alternative to traditional space-based interceptors (SBIs). It is presented as a potential solution for space defense.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce proposed a one-stop licensing system for novel space missions to streamline regulatory authority. This initiative aims to address gaps in existing frameworks for missions that don't fit traditional categories.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space has secured $40 million in funding to advance its in-space propulsion technology, which uses storable bipropellant to simplify and reduce the cost of launch operations compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase, a new startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation for the growing number of space missions. The company aims to address the increasing demand for spectrum management as orbital activities expand.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has shifted priorities to foster a commercially driven lunar economy with surface infrastructure. A detailed plan has been developed to support this new vision.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler discussed Northwood Space's efforts to address satellite ground infrastructure challenges by creating an end-to-end ground system designed for rapid deployment. The initiative aims to streamline satellite operations through innovative infrastructure solutions.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The conversation highlights advancements in this technology.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market outlook amid volatility, with some noting high valuations and sovereign demand dynamics. The discussion focused on navigating challenges like direct-to-direct (D2D) strategies and economic fluctuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discussed the company's RF solutions, including phased arrays for LEO satellites, during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Qorvo's advancements in satellite communication technology.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are increasingly prioritizing sovereign satellite communications networks to enhance data security amid rising geopolitical tensions. The challenge lies in defining the scope and capabilities of sovereign space infrastructure in a complex global environment.

title: Investors Debate IPO Path, Financing Routes Needed to Grow Space Market
summary: Investors are discussing the need for better financing routes, such as IPOs, to support the growing commercial space sector. Venture capitalists at a SATShow panel highlighted the gap between industry advancements and financing capabilities.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for efficiently downlinking space-collected data to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the space industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have embraced inflight connectivity (IFC) as a standard offering for passengers over the past two decades, with growing demand driving further development in the sector.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulations are identified as key trends driving the growth of the space economy in India and the Asia-Pacific region. The role of governments as investors and market creators is expected to dominate the sector's development.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems at SATShow Week, featuring live multi-orbit demonstrations.
